claim: IEA SDG7 Tracking reports over 600 million people in Sub-Saharan Africa lack electricity access, rural electrification rates below 30 percent, and mini-grids identified as least-cost solution for over 40 percent of new connections needed by 2030.

source.title: Tracking SDG7: The Energy Progress Report 2024

method: Reviewed SDG7 report chapters on electricity access, mini-grid projections, and investment needs.
limitations: Access data is modeled from surveys of variable quality.
Least-cost classification depends on demand assumptions.

claim: Solar PV costs have fallen 90 percent since 2010, solar-hybrid mini-grid levelized cost of electricity is below diesel for most rural communities, and mini-grid installed costs are projected to fall further with scale.

source.title: Mini Grids for Half a Billion People

method: Reviewed World Bank report on mini-grid costs, deployment projections, and policy recommendations.
limitations: Cost projections assume supportive regulatory environments.
Actual costs vary substantially by country and site conditions.
